---
title: "Ancestry-Aware PRS with genoformerR (v0.2)"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Ancestry-Aware PRS with genoformerR}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE, echo = TRUE)
```

## What's new in v0.2

v0.2 adds **local ancestry conditioning** for admixed cohorts:

| Feature | v0.1 | v0.2 |
|---|---|---|
| Global ancestry (FiLM) | ✅ | ✅ |
| Per-SNP local ancestry embedding | ❌ | ✅ (RFMix2) |
| Haplotype phasing wrapper | ❌ | ✅ (SHAPEIT4) |
| Graceful global-only fallback | ✅ | ✅ |
| `GenoFormerPhased` (bi-haplotype) | ❌ | ✅ (experimental) |

## Quick start — global-only (no phasing required)

```r
library(genoformerR)
gf_init()

results <- gf_run_pipeline(
  dosage_raw = "data/plink/kg3_pgs_dosage.raw",
  pgs_file   = "PGS000018.txt.gz",
  panel_file = "data/1kg/integrated_call_samples_v3.20130502.ALL.panel",
  phenotype  = pheno_vec,
  outdir     = "results/global_only"
  # run_local_anc = FALSE is the default
)
```

## Full dual-level pipeline (recommended for AMR/admixed cohorts)

```r
library(genoformerR)
gf_init()

results <- gf_run_pipeline(
  dosage_raw       = "data/plink/kg3_pgs_dosage.raw",
  pgs_file         = "PGS000018.txt.gz",
  panel_file       = "data/1kg/integrated_call_samples_v3.20130502.ALL.panel",
  phenotype        = pheno_vec,
  outdir           = "results/dual_prs",
  # Local ancestry options
  run_phasing      = TRUE,                  # runs SHAPEIT4
  run_local_anc    = TRUE,                  # runs RFMix2
  gmap_dir         = "data/genetic_maps",
  ref_vcf          = "data/1kg/kg3_phased.vcf.gz",
  rfmix_sample_map = "data/rfmix_sample_map.txt",
  k_pops           = 5L,                    # K reference pops in RFMix2
  model_config     = list(d_model = 256, n_layers = 6, d_local = 64),
  train_config     = list(epochs = 100, batch_size = 64)
)

# Portability plot
results$evaluation$portability_plot

# Local ancestry summary across chromosomes
summary_tbl <- gf_local_anc_summary(
  results$local_anc_arr,
  snp_chroms  = chroms_snp,
  pop_labels  = results$ancestry$labels
)
```

## Step-by-step: local ancestry only

If you already have phased VCFs (e.g. UK Biobank post-phasing):

```r
gf_init()

# Step 1: Run RFMix2 on pre-phased VCFs
rfmix_out <- gf_local_ancestry(
  phased_vcfs  = c("1" = "chr1_phased.vcf.gz", "2" = "chr2_phased.vcf.gz"),
  ref_vcf      = "kg3_phased.vcf.gz",
  sample_map   = "rfmix_sample_map.txt",
  outdir       = "rfmix_output",
  gmap_dir     = "genetic_maps",
  k_pops       = 5L
)

# Step 2: Load and align posteriors to your SNP order
local_anc_arr <- gf_load_local_anc(
  rfmix_prefixes    = rfmix_out,
  snp_ids           = colnames(dosage_mat),
  snp_positions     = snp_pos,
  snp_chroms        = snp_chr,
  sample_ids        = rownames(dosage_mat),
  global_anc        = anc$anc_probs,    # fallback for non-admixed samples
  entropy_threshold = 0.3               # samples below this use global anc
)

dim(local_anc_arr)  # N_samples × N_snps × K_pops
```

## Architecture notes

### Why local ancestry in the token, not the block?

Global ancestry is a single vector per individual — it makes sense to broadcast
it to every block via FiLM. Local ancestry is a *sequence* (one K-vector per SNP
position), so it must be incorporated at the token level before attention
computes position-to-position interactions. This ordering means attention heads
can learn ancestry-stratified LD: a head attending from a EUR-tagged SNP to
another EUR-tagged SNP can up-weight that interaction relative to a cross-ancestry
pair.

### Entropy-based fallback

For samples with low admixture entropy (< `entropy_threshold`), local ancestry
inference is unreliable — RFMix2 switches rapidly between ancestry states due
to noise rather than real mosaic structure. The fallback broadcasts their global
ancestry vector across all SNP positions, which is functionally equivalent to
v0.1 behaviour for those samples. Admixed samples (AMR, African American, etc.)
with high entropy receive full local ancestry conditioning.

### `GenoFormerPhased` (experimental)

For datasets where phased haplotypes are available and per-haplotype RFMix2
posteriors have been computed, `GenoFormerPhased` processes hap0 and hap1
through shared model weights and sums their PRS contributions. This is strictly
more informative than diploid averaging and captures phase-dependent effects,
but doubles memory consumption. Use via Python directly from `model.py`.

## RFMix2 sample map format

The `rfmix_sample_map` file is a headerless 2-column TSV: reference sample ID
and population label. Only reference panel samples (not study samples) should
appear here.

```
HG00096    EUR
HG00171    EUR
HG01880    AFR
NA19701    AMR
...
```

Population labels should match those you want K to represent. For a Latino
cohort, typical K=3: `EUR`, `AFR`, `NAT` (Native American proxy from 1KGP3
PEL/MXL).

## References

- Maples et al. (2013) *AJHG* — RFMix algorithm
- Cabreros et al. (2023) — RFMix2
- Delaneau et al. (2019) *Nature Communications* — SHAPEIT4
- Martin et al. (2019) *Nature Genetics* — PRS portability
- Ruan et al. (2022) *Nature Medicine* — multi-ancestry PRSice
